name: Printing Press (Movable Type)
tagline: Mechanised impression of reusable type — mass duplication of text with repeatable fidelity
problem:
Scriptoria copied manuscripts slowly; errors compounded; cost limited books to institutions. Movable type cuts marginal cost per page after the first composition: each character is a durable punch, cast in alloy, arranged into a forme, inked, and pressed uniformly.

The engineering problems are mechanical: even impression across a wide platen, ink viscosity that neither dries on type nor bleeds on paper, and alloy hardness that survives millions of impacts. The information problem is workflow: justification, proofing, and distribution of corrected editions.

Printing did not invent text — it industrialised fidelity and speed, enabling law, science, and heresy to travel faster than censors could rewrite by hand.
overview:
Punches cut in steel are struck into copper matrices; type is cast in lead-tin-antimony alloy with a shoulder for locking. Compositors set lines in composing sticks; furniture and quoins lock the chase. The press supplies gradual pressure via screw or later lever and toggle mechanisms; platen or cylinder designs evolve toward rotary newspaper presses.

Oil-based inks coat metal type better than water-based on greasy fingers — formulation becomes chemistry. Paper must withstand squeeze without embossing through; dampening helps some processes.

Post-Gutenberg evolution added stereotype plates, linotype slug casting, and offset lithography — but movable type is the conceptual fork between copying and manufacturing text.

Digital fonts are coordinate lists — intellectual descendants of the punchcutter’s file.

principles.name: Viscosity and tack
principles.explanation: Ink must split cleanly between type surface and paper without picking fibres — temperature and pigment load tune rheology.

principles.name: Hardness matching
principles.explanation: Type metal softer than punch steel but harder than paper contaminants — otherwise letters mushroom.

principles.name: Impression pressure distribution
principles.explanation: Platen parallelism and packing paper shims equalise density — else outer lines fade.

principles.name: Justification geometry
principles.explanation: Word spacing and thin spaces absorb line-length slack — hyphenation rules are hyphenation algorithms.

rawMaterials.name: Type metal
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: 3D-printed polymer type for short runs — wears fast

rawMaterials.name: Punch steel
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Harden and temper; polish striking face

buildSteps.title: Cut and justify punches
buildSteps.description: File counters; proof on smoke-blackened paper; strike matrix.

buildSteps.title: Cast type to height
buildSteps.description: Measure type height to press standard; shave feet if needed.
buildSteps.warningNote: Mixed heights crack formes under pressure

buildSteps.title: Compose and lock
buildSteps.description: Insert leads; lock with quoins; proof on waste sheets.

buildSteps.title: Ink and pull
buildSteps.description: Charge rollers evenly; two-pull habit for solids; inspect register.

buildSteps.title: Distribute and maintain
buildSteps.description: Return sorts to cases; melt worn type; document font inventory.

history.event: Bi Sheng experiments with ceramic movable type in Chinese tradition.
Gutenberg Bible era printing with metal type and press in Mainz.

inventors.name: Johannes Gutenberg
inventors.contribution: European metal movable-type printing with oil ink and press

impact:
Printing collapsed the price of identical knowledge — law, scripture, and diagrams could disagree in public because multiple copies existed. It preconditioned scientific societies and newspapers.

Movable type’s social externalities include propaganda at scale — technology never prints only truth.

videos.title: Letterpress printing
videos.description: Museum demonstrations of composition and pull.
